description: A traditional British dish of battered and fried fish served with thick-cut fries. It is commonly accompanied by tartar sauce, lemon, mushy peas, or malt vinegar.
core_ingredients: White fish such as cod or haddock
preparation_steps: Fry the potatoes until cooked and crisp.
Fry the fish until golden and flaky inside.
Serve hot with chips and common accompaniments.

health_advice.benefits: Provides protein from the fish and can be filling as a meal.
health_advice.risks: Deep frying increases fat and calories, and salted sides can add significant sodium.
health_advice.healthy_tip: For a lighter meal, share the portion or pair the fish with salad and less fried potato.

dietary_notes.note: Gluten-free versions are possible only if the batter is made without wheat.
